html:
In this unit we cover boolean operators: AND (&&), OR (||), and NOT (!), along with comparison operators (<=, >=, ==, !=).

You will learn to evaluate boolean expressions, build truth tables, and apply operator precedence rules when combining conditions.

Key concepts:

  • De Morgan's laws: !(A && B) is equivalent to (!A || !B)
  • Short-circuit evaluation: Java stops evaluating as soon as the result is determined
  • Range conditions: to check if x is between 5 and 10 inclusive, use 5 <= x && x <= 10
